title: Spring Boot Intro
content:
Spring Boot Introduction

The Opinionated Framework

Spring Boot is an open-source Java-based framework used to create a microservice. It is developed by Pivotal Team and is used to build stand-alone and production-ready spring applications. It is famous for its "Opinionated" approach, which simplifies the configuration of Spring-based applications by providing sensible defaults.

title: Spring Initializr
content:
Spring Initializr

Bootstrapping Your Project

The easiest way to create a Spring Boot project is using Spring Initializr. This web tool allows you to select your build tool (Maven/Gradle), language (Java/Kotlin/Groovy), Spring Boot version, and dependencies needed for your application.

Common Dependencies
  • Spring Web: For building RESTful APIs.
  • Spring Data JPA: For database access.
  • Spring Boot Actuator: For monitoring.
  • Lombok: To reduce boilerplate code.

title: Project Structure
content:
Project Structure

Anatomy of a Project

A typical Spring Boot Maven project follows a standard directory structure:

Directory Layout
src/
  main/
    java/           # Java source files
      com.example.demo/
        DemoApplication.java  # Main entry point
    resources/      # Configuration and static assets
      application.properties  # App configuration
      static/       # CSS, JS, images
      templates/    # HTML templates (Thymeleaf)
  test/             # Unit and integration tests
pom.xml             # Maven dependencies

title: Hello Controller
content:
Hello Controller

Creating an Endpoint

In Spring Boot, you use @RestController to mark a class as a controller where every method returns a domain object instead of a view.

HelloController.java
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;

@RestController
public class HelloController {

    @GetMapping("/hello")
    public String sayHello() {
        return "Hello from Spring Boot!";
    }
}

title: Request Mapping
content:
Request Mapping

Handling HTTP Methods

Spring Boot provides specific annotations for different HTTP methods, making the code more readable.

  • @GetMapping: For reading data (GET).
  • @PostMapping: For creating data (POST).
  • @PutMapping: For updating data (PUT).
  • @DeleteMapping: For deleting data (DELETE).
  • @PatchMapping: For partial updates (PATCH).

title: Dependency Injection
content:
Dependency Injection

@Autowired and Beans

Spring Boot's core feature is Dependency Injection. You define classes as @Component, @Service, or @Repository, and Spring automatically manages their lifecycle as "Beans".

Constructor Injection
@Service
public class MessageService {
    public String getMessage() { return "Injected Service"; }
}

@RestController
public class MyController {
    private final MessageService service;

    // Constructor injection is the recommended way
    public MyController(MessageService service) {
        this.service = service;
    }
}

title: Spring Data JPA
content:
Spring Data JPA

Repository Pattern

Spring Data JPA reduces boilerplate code by providing a repository abstraction. You just need to define an interface extending JpaRepository.

UserRepository.java
import org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository;
import org.springframework.stereotype.Repository;

@Repository
public interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User, Long> {
    // Spring generates implementations for standard CRUD automatically!
    User findByUsername(String username);
}

title: Auto Configuration
content:
Auto-Configuration

Sensible Defaults

Spring Boot's auto-configuration attempts to automatically configure your Spring application based on the jar dependencies that you have added. For example, if H2 is on your classpath and you haven't manually configured any database connection beans, then Spring Boot auto-configures an in-memory database.

The @SpringBootApplication Annotation

This single annotation includes @Configuration, @EnableAutoConfiguration, and @ComponentScan.

title: Properties
content:
Application Properties

External Configuration

You can use application.properties or application.yml to configure your application port, database URL, and custom values.

application.properties
server.port=8081
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/mydb
my.custom.property=Hello World
Using @Value
@Value("${my.custom.property}")
private String message;

title: Actuator
content:
Spring Boot Actuator

Monitoring & Metrics

Actuator brings production-ready features to our application. It is used to monitor and manage the application through HTTP endpoints.

  • /health: Shows application health information.
  • /metrics: Shows various metrics information.
  • /env: Returns list of properties in Spring’s ConfigurableEnvironment.
  • /loggers: Shows and modifies the configuration of loggers.

title: DevTools
content:
Spring Boot DevTools

Developer Productivity

Spring Boot DevTools is a set of tools that can make the development process much easier. The most important feature is Automatic Restart: whenever files on the classpath change, the application automatically restarts.

Maven Dependency
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
  <optional>true</optional>
</dependency>

title: Profiles
content:
Profiles

Environment Specific Config

Spring Profiles provide a way to segregate parts of your application configuration and make it only available in certain environments (e.g., dev, test, prod).

application-dev.properties
server.port=8081
logging.level.org.springframework=DEBUG

You can activate a profile using spring.profiles.active=dev in your main properties file.

title: Error Handling
content:
Error Handling

Global Exception Handler

Spring Boot provides a clean way to handle exceptions globally using @ControllerAdvice and @ExceptionHandler.

GlobalExceptionHandler.java
@ControllerAdvice
public class GlobalExceptionHandler {

    @ExceptionHandler(ResourceNotFoundException.class)
    public ResponseEntity<?> handleNotFound(ResourceNotFoundException ex) {
        return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND).body(ex.getMessage());
    }
}

title: Validation
content:
Validation

Data Integrity

You can use JSR-303/JSR-380 annotations to validate incoming data in your controllers.

User.java
public class User {
    @NotNull(message = "Name is required")
    private String name;

    @Email(message = "Invalid email")
    private String email;

    @Min(18)
    private int age;
}

Use @Valid in your controller to trigger validation.

title: Spring Security
content:
Spring Security

Securing Your App

Spring Security is a powerful and highly customizable authentication and access-control framework. Just adding the dependency secures all endpoints by default with Basic Auth.

SecurityConfig.java
@Configuration
@EnableWebSecurity
public class SecurityConfig extends WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter {
    @Override
    protected void configure(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
        http.authorizeRequests()
            .antMatchers("/public/**").permitAll()
            .anyRequest().authenticated()
            .and().formLogin();
    }
}

title: Logging
content:
Logging

Tracking Events

Spring Boot uses SLF4J with Logback as the default logging provider. You can easily log messages at different levels (TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR).

Logger Example
private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyController.class);

@GetMapping("/")
public String index() {
    logger.info("Index page accessed");
    return "Hello";
}

title: Testing
content:
Testing

Quality Assurance

Spring Boot provides @SpringBootTest for integration testing, which starts the entire application context. For unit testing slices, you can use @WebMvcTest or @DataJpaTest.

SampleTest.java
@SpringBootTest
class DemoApplicationTests {
    @Test
    void contextLoads() {
    }
}

title: Production Build
content:
Production Build

Deploying Your App

Building a Spring Boot application for production typically results in an "Executable JAR" that contains all dependencies, including the embedded server.

Maven Build Command
mvn clean package

You can then run the app with: java -jar demo-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar

title: Spring Boot Summary
content:
Spring Boot Summary

Ready for Production

Spring Boot has revolutionized Java web development by providing a fast, easy, and opinionated way to build production-grade applications.

  • Simplified Config: Auto-configuration and starters.
  • Embedded Server: No need for external Tomcat installation.
  • Microservices Ready: Perfect for small, independent services.
  • Security & Monitoring: Built-in support for security and actuator.
  • Ecosystem: Seamless integration with Spring Data, Spring Security, and Cloud.

You are now ready to build scalable, full-stack enterprise applications using Spring Boot!
